sourceMovie.title: Toy Story 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Toy Story 2 (1999) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Toy Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Toy Story 2 clicked for you, Toy Story (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Toy Story 2?
faq.answer: Try Toy Story, Toy Story 3, Moana—each shares DNA with Toy Story 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Toy Story 2?
faq.answer: Toy Story 2 (1999) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Toy Story 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Toy Story Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
